verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (TMP21/p24delta1) is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein of the p24/TMED family. The NCBI summary explicitly notes it is localized to both the plasma membrane AND Golgi cisternae, placing it in dual_localization: it cycles between Golgi/ER and the cell surface as part of its vesicular trafficking role. Its extracellular GOLD domain faces the lumen/extracellular space, making it accessible from the outer leaflet when at the PM. The dominant functional context in literature is Golgi/ERGIC trafficking, but a documented PM pool exists, qualifying as contextual with dual_localization.
